chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Presents: Mr. Justice Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro Mr. Justice Khalid Hussain Shahani Cr.B.A.No.631 of 2026 Kamran Khan.........................................Applicant Vs. The State...............................................Respondent For hearing of bail application 07.04.2026 Mr. Tajjamul H.Lodhi advocate for the applicant a/w Nousheen Khan Advocate. Mrs. Firdous Faridi, advocate for Additional Collector of Customs Collectorate of Customs Airports Karachi. Asif Jmal (Preventive Officer) ----------- O R D E R Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro, J:- Applicant is seeking post arrest bail in case bearing Crime No.P-34/2025-IMO, U/s 6/9 8,14 & 15 of CNS Act, 1997 (as amended in 2022), registered at Police Station Custom Karachi. 2. Allegedly, customs officials found a suspicious parcel at Jinnah Airport International Mail Office and took its possession on 09.12.2025 and from its search recovered 10226 MDMA tablets weighing 4.280 Kgs. In investigation, the Custom official found that the said parcel was sent by Bashir Ahmadi from Germany, hence an FIR was registered bearing Crime No.34 of 2025 on the same date. 3. On 05.01.2026, allegedly applicant came to enquire about that parcel and hence was arrested. In the investigation, he disclosed that he was sent there by his friend Naeem to make an enquiry regarding arrival of the parcel. A controlled communication was established between him and Naeem in which it was discovered that the master mind of the transportation was Sher Muhammad, who was subsequently arrested, whose mobile phone number had already been found on the said parcel.

In the investigation, he disclosed that he was sent there by his friend Naeem to make an enquiry regarding arrival of the parcel. A controlled communication was established between him and Naeem in which it was discovered that the master mind of the transportation was Sher Muhammad, who was subsequently arrested, whose mobile phone number had already been found on the said parcel. On the basis of such investigation, the applicant has been accordingly arraigned in the case. 2 4. Learned counsel for the applicant has pleaded for bail on the ground that applicant had gone to the office of Custom to just make an enquiry without any knowledge of contents of the parcel. 5. Learned Special Prosecutor Customs and investigating officer have opposed bail stating that applicant is an accomplice in the crime. 6. Be that as it may, the only allegations against the applicant is of making an enquiry regarding the arrival of the parcel. Prima-facie there is no evidence showing that applicant was in knowledge of the contents of the parcel or he was in league with the main accused. Prima-facie, on account of his co-operation in the investigation, the Custom Officials have been able to identify the main accused. Therefore, we find the case against the applicant to be of further enquiry and allow this bail application. Resultantly, the applicant is granted bail subject to furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.200,000/- and P.R. bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the trial Court. Bail application stands disposed of. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and would not prejudice case of ether party at trial. JUDGE JUDGE Imran
